I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): The type just added `'stateful'` to the union. The existing values `submitBtnVariant ?? 'primary'` and `"link"` are still valid. No code change needed — this is purely additive.

**Fix 2** (Modal family migration): 
- `footer` prop (line 96-138) → Move content to `<ModalFooter>` as children
- `title={headerText}` (line 145) → Move to `<ModalHeader title={headerText} />`
- `titleIconVariant={titleIconVariant}` (line 146) → Move to `<ModalHeader titleIconVariant={titleIconVariant} />`
- `onClose` (line 143) → stays on Modal
- Need to add `ModalBody`, `ModalHeader`, `ModalFooter` imports

**Fix 3** (Button icon children to prop): Looking at lines 117-124 and 127-129:
- Line 117-124: Button contains only text `{submitBtnText || t('Save')}` — no icon. No change needed.
- Line 127-129: Button contains only text `{t('Cancel')}` — no icon. No change needed.

**Fix 4** (StackItem `component` prop): The `component` prop on `StackItem` is for setting the HTML element type (like `div`, `section`, etc.), not for absorbing children content. The existing `StackItem` usages are fine — they use children correctly. No change needed.

Let me now apply the changes:
Now let me verify the result:
The file looks correct. Let me verify there are no companion test files to check:
No test files in the directory. Let me also search more broadly for test files that reference TabModal:

No companion test files found. The changes are complete.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — Button.variant type changed (`'stateful'` added to union)
**No change needed.** The type change is purely additive (added `'stateful'` to the variant union). The existing values `submitBtnVariant ?? 'primary'` and `"link"` remain valid in the new type. No code modification required.

### Fix 2 — Modal family migration (v5 → v6 composition)
**Applied.** Made the following changes:
- **Added imports**: `ModalBody`, `ModalFooter`, `ModalHeader` from `@patternfly/react-core`
- **Removed `footer` prop** from `<Modal>`: The entire JSX block (error alert + action buttons) was moved out of the `footer` prop and placed inside a `<ModalFooter>` child component
- **Removed `title` prop** from `<Modal>`: Moved `title={headerText}` to `<ModalHeader title={headerText} />`
- **Removed `titleIconVariant` prop** from `<Modal>`: Moved `titleIconVariant={titleIconVariant}` to the same `<ModalHeader>` component
- **Added `<ModalBody>`**: Wrapped the existing `{children}` content in `<ModalBody>`
- **Props preserved on `<Modal>`**: `className`, `id`, `isOpen`, `onClose`, `position`, `variant` — all remain unchanged

### Fix 3 — Button icon children to `icon` prop
**No change needed.** Neither Button instance contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The first Button (line 117) contains only text `{submitBtnText || t('Save')}`, and the second Button (line 127) contains only text `{t('Cancel')}`. No icon-to-prop migration is applicable.

### Fix 4 — StackItem `component` prop
**No change needed.** The `component` prop on `StackItem` is for setting the rendered HTML element type (e.g., `div`, `section`), not for absorbing children content. All existing `StackItem` usages correctly pass content as children (error message, alert, action list, links). No migration is appropriate here.
